mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand, which boosts real GDP growth expectations and inflation anticipations, leading to higher long-term yields as investors price in stronger economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales increased in late 2012, supporting recovery narrative.
failure_condition: 20-day yield changes are negative or zero in more than half of periods when RSAFS rises month-over-month over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real bond returns and prompts expectations of future Fed tightening, causing the 10-year yield to rise as investors demand higher nominal compensation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI level at 231.0 as of latest reading.
failure_condition: Rank IC of CPIAUCSL acceleration vs. 60-day yield return is below 0.05 over any 3-year period, or yield falls in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth signals labor market tightening, while the Fed funds rate remains near zero, creating an expectation of eventual normalization. The combination amplifies upward pressure on yields as markets anticipate reduced accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls rose 155k in December 2012; Fed funds at 0.16%.
regime_hint: zero lower bound economy with improving labor market
failure_condition: The joint signal (rising PAYEMS + low FEDFUNDS) fails to produce positive yield returns in >55% of cases over a 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts point to a recovery in residential investment and household wealth. In the post-crisis regime, rising starts reinforce growth expectations and push yields higher as the economy shifts from repair to expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts reached 861k in November 2012, above 800k.
regime_hint: housing market recovery after financial crisis
failure_condition: In periods when HOUST > 800, the 20-day yield return is negative more often than positive (p < 0.10 in binomial test) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in industrial production signals robust supply-side momentum, which lifts output gap estimates and nominal growth expectations, leading to higher yields as real rates rise.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index at 97.5, recent trend modestly positive.
failure_condition: 5-day yield returns following IP surges are negative in >60% of cases over a 1-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Meaningful declines in the unemployment rate tighten labor market conditions and raise wage inflation expectations. Markets respond by pricing higher long-term yields, particularly over a quarterly horizon as the trend becomes embedded.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.2 percentage points over three months
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment fell from 7.9% to 7.8% in December 2012.
failure_condition: 60-day yield returns are negative in more than half of instances when the 3-month UNRATE decline condition triggers over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales and industrial production rise simultaneously, it confirms a broad-based economic expansion that strongly raises growth expectations. The combined signal amplifies the upward pressure on yields beyond what each series would predict independently.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and INDPRO post positive monthly growth
evidence_refs: fact:Recent monthly data show both series increasing.
regime_hint: synchronized expansion in consumption and production
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS*INDPRO) in a linear model fails to have significant positive coefficient (t-stat < 1.64) for 5-day yield changes over a 1-year evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A rising consumer price index combined with a near-zero federal funds rate generates a negative real rate environment. As inflation expectations adjust upward, investors sell long-duration bonds, pushing yields higher.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI Y/Y approx 1.7% in latest data; Fed funds at 0.16%.
failure_condition: When CPI > 2% and Fed funds < 1%, the 20-day yield change is negative or insignificant in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year sample.
